The creation process, it is breaking down the steps that are necessary to outline the content, create a brief for understanding what information we need to gather, then a research base to go out there and find research, whether that's with LLMs or with knowledge bases or usually a combination of both.

The client gives us information they want, and we also integrate information from public research, and then building drafts of the article and then refinements after the draft, linking, fact-checking, that sort of thing.

but...

We're taking the approach to it that we want to hire people that understand the way to do that, to build solid quality content, but then also have the ability to articulate through the breaking down of the processes that they're doing, the bits that normally happen in here.

No, I'm going to discard these two options and choose this one.
You know, those processes happen a lot of times up here, especially as you're writing or drafting and going, oh, these are the topics that I want to cover and doing that.

Or how to build an automation or workflow that can help them to do that.
So just to provide that overall context, we definitely want to bring on people with content chops, but it can't end there.
